Now I'll create the sequence diagram to illustrate the program call flow for the Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway web interface.

```json
[
  {
    "command_name": "Editor.write",
    "args": {
      "path": "jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_sequence_diagram.mermaid",
      "content": "sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Web Client
    participant API as API Server
    participant Auth as AuthService
    participant Dashboard as DashboardService
    participant Stats as SMSStatistics
    participant CLI as JasminCLIAdapter
    participant HTTP as HttpAPIClient
    participant WS as WebSocketManager
    participant DB as Database
    participant Jasmin as Jasmin SMPP Gateway

    %% User Authentication Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/auth/login (credentials)
    API->>Auth: login(username, password)
    Auth->>DB: validateCredentials(username, password)
    DB-->>Auth: user object or error
    Auth-->>API: JWT token or error
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (token) or 401 Unauthorized

    %% Dashboard Loading Flow
    Client->>API: GET /api/dashboard (token)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user or error
    API->>Dashboard: getSystemOverview()
    Dashboard->>Stats: collectSystemStats(timeRange)
    Stats->>DB: queryStatistics(params)
    DB-->>Stats: statistics data
    Dashboard->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -l")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>Dashboard: parsed connector list
    Dashboard-->>API: compiled dashboard data
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (dashboard data)

    %% SMPP Connector Management Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/connectors (connector details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -a")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveConnectorDetails(connector)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error
    API->>WS: publishUpdate("connectors", { action: "created", data: connector })
    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (new connector)

    %% Start/Stop Connector Flow
    Client->>API: PUT /api/connectors/:id/status (action)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -1 {id} -S")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: updateConnectorStatus(id, status)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 200 OK or error
    API->>WS: publishUpdate("connectors", { action: "updated", data: connector })
    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (status change)

    %% Routing Management Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/routes (route details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("morouter -a")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveRouteDetails(route)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error

    %% SMS Sending Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/sms/send (message details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>HTTP: sendSMS(params)
    HTTP->>Jasmin: HTTP API request
    Jasmin-->>HTTP: message ID or error
    HTTP-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveMessageDetails(message)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 202 Accepted or error

    %% Real-time Statistics Update
    Jasmin->>Stats: message status update
    Stats->>DB: updateStatistics(data)
    Stats->>WS: publishUpdate("statistics", newData)
    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (new statistics)

    %% User Management Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/users (user details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated admin user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("user -a")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveUserDetails(user)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error

    %% Configuration Backup Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/config/backup
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated admin user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("persist")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveBackupRecord(backup)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (backup ID)

    %% Statistics Report Generation
    Client->>API: GET /api/reports/generate (parameters)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>Stats: generateReport(params)
    Stats->>DB: queryStatistics(params)
    DB-->>Stats: statistics data
    Stats-->>API: formatted report
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (report data/PDF)"
    }
  }
]
```

Now that I've read the PRD, I'll design a comprehensive system architecture for the Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway web interface. I'll focus on creating a design that meets all the requirements while ensuring the architecture is simple, maintainable, and leverages appropriate open-source libraries.

```json
[
  {
    "command_name": "Editor.write",
    "args": {
      "path": "jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_system_design.md",
      "content": "# Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway Web Interface: System Design

## Implementation Approach

Based on the PRD requirements, we need to design a web interface that provides complete access to Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway's CLI features while adding user-specific statistics tracking. The architecture must support real-time monitoring, comprehensive user management, and intuitive visualization of SMS traffic data.

### Key Technical Challenges

1. **CLI Command Integration**: We need to seamlessly translate web interface actions to Jasmin CLI commands and interpret their responses.
2. **Real-time Monitoring**: The system must provide real-time updates on SMPP connections, message queues, and system status.
3. **Statistics Collection and Storage**: We need an efficient way to collect, store, and query SMS statistics without impacting gateway performance.
4. **Authentication and Authorization**: The system requires secure, role-based access control that integrates with Jasmin's user management.
5. **Scalable Architecture**: The design must accommodate growing SMS traffic volumes and user base.

### Technology Selection

1. **Frontend**: 
   - React with TypeScript for type safety and enhanced development experience
   - Redux Toolkit for state management
   - Tailwind CSS for responsive UI
   - React Query for data fetching and caching
   - Chart.js and D3.js for data visualization
   - Socket.IO client for real-time updates

2. **Backend**:
   - Node.js with Express for the web server
   - Socket.IO for WebSocket connections
   - Passport.js for authentication
   - Prisma ORM for database interaction
   - Bull for job queuing and scheduling

3. **Database**:
   - PostgreSQL for relational data (users, configurations)
   - TimescaleDB extension for time-series data (statistics)

4. **Jasmin Integration**:
   - Custom adapter for Jasmin's Management PB API
   - HTTP client for Jasmin's HTTP API
   - Custom telnet client for CLI command execution

5. **Deployment**:
   - Docker and Docker Compose for containerization
   - GitHub Actions for CI/CD
   - Nginx as reverse proxy

## Data Structures and Interfaces

The system architecture consists of several key components organized in a layered approach:

1. **Presentation Layer**: React frontend application
2. **API Layer**: Express.js REST API
3. **Service Layer**: Core business logic and integrations
4. **Data Layer**: Databases and data access
5. **Integration Layer**: Jasmin SMPP Gateway interaction

Each component is designed with clear interfaces and responsibilities, following clean architecture principles to ensure separation of concerns and testability.

## System Components

### 1. Frontend Application

The React-based frontend provides an intuitive interface for all Jasmin SMPP Gateway management tasks. Key features include:

- Responsive dashboard with live statistics
- User and group management forms
- Connector configuration interfaces
- Visual route builder
- Interactive charts for SMS traffic analysis

### 2. Backend API Server

The Express.js backend serves as the central coordination point, handling:

- REST API endpoints for frontend requests
- Authentication and authorization
- WebSocket connections for real-time updates
- Request validation and error handling
- Statistics aggregation and reporting

### 3. Jasmin Integration Service

This service handles all interactions with the Jasmin SMPP Gateway:

- CLI command execution via telnet
- Management PB API integration
- HTTP API client for message operations
- Connection monitoring
- Configuration management

### 4. Statistics Collection Service

Responsible for gathering, processing, and storing SMS traffic statistics:

- Message throughput tracking
- User-specific statistics collection
- Delivery status monitoring
- Performance metrics aggregation
- Report generation

### 5. Database Systems

The data storage layer consists of:

- PostgreSQL for user data, configurations, and system settings
- TimescaleDB for time-series statistics data
- Redis for caching and session management

## Integration with Jasmin SMPP Gateway

The system integrates with Jasmin SMPP Gateway through multiple channels:

1. **Telnet Interface**: For executing CLI commands and receiving responses
2. **HTTP API**: For sending/receiving SMS messages and simple operations
3. **Management PB API**: For advanced management operations
4. **Log Files**: For collecting additional diagnostic information
5. **Custom Metrics Collection**: For gathering detailed statistics

## Authentication and Authorization

The system implements a comprehensive security model:

1. **User Authentication**: JWT-based authentication with secure password handling
2. **Role-Based Access Control**: Admin, Manager, and User roles with appropriate permissions
3. **Session Management**: Secure session handling with appropriate timeouts
4. **API Security**: HTTPS enforcement, rate limiting, and input validation

## Scalability Considerations

The architecture is designed to scale horizontally:

1. **Stateless API Servers**: Multiple instances can operate behind a load balancer
2. **Separate Statistics Processing**: Background workers handle statistics collection
3. **Database Optimization**: Indexes and partitioning for efficient queries
4. **Caching Strategy**: Redis-based caching for frequently accessed data

## Deployment Architecture

The system is containerized using Docker for easy deployment and scaling:

1. **Frontend Container**: Serves the React application
2. **API Server Container**: Handles REST API requests
3. **Worker Container**: Processes background jobs and statistics collection
4. **Database Containers**: PostgreSQL and Redis
5. **Nginx Container**: Acts as reverse proxy and static file server

## Monitoring and Logging

Comprehensive monitoring ensures system health and performance:

1. **Application Logging**: Structured logs with appropriate log levels
2. **Performance Metrics**: CPU, memory, and response time tracking
3. **Error Tracking**: Centralized error collection and notification
4. **Audit Trail**: Tracking of administrative actions

## Anything UNCLEAR

1. **Jasmin Version Compatibility**: The design assumes compatibility with recent Jasmin SMPP Gateway versions. Specific version requirements should be clarified.
2. **Performance Requirements**: Specific throughput expectations would help refine the architecture for scale.
3. **Data Retention Policies**: Clarification on how long statistics data should be retained would guide database design decisions.
4. **Integration with External Systems**: Requirements for integrating with external billing or monitoring systems should be explored further.
5. **Deployment Environment**: Details about the target deployment environment would help optimize resource allocation and configuration."
    }
  }
]
```

```json
[
  {
    "command_name": "Editor.write",
    "args": {
      "path": "jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_class_diagram.mermaid",
      "content": "classDiagram
    class User {
        +string id
        +string username
        +string passwordHash
        +string email
        +string[] roles
        +boolean isActive
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
        +authenticate(password: string): boolean
        +hasPermission(permission: string): boolean
    }
    
    class Group {
        +string id
        +string name
        +string description
        +User[] members
        +Object permissions
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
        +addUser(user: User): void
        +removeUser(user: User): void
        +hasPermission(permission: string): boolean
    }
    
    class SMPPConnector {
        +string id
        +string name
        +string host
        +number port
        +string systemId
        +string password
        +string systemType
        +ConnectionStatus status
        +Object advancedParams
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
        +connect(): Promise~boolean~
        +disconnect(): Promise~boolean~
        +getStatus(): ConnectionStatus
        +updateConfig(params: Object): Promise~boolean~
    }
    
    class Route {
        +string id
        +string type %% MO or MT
        +string connectorId
        +Filter[] filters
        +number priority
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
        +matchesMessage(message: SMSMessage): boolean
        +applyRoute(message: SMSMessage): Promise~boolean~
    }
    
    class Filter {
        +string id
        +string type %% e.g., destination_number, source_number, content
        +string operator %% e.g., equal, contains, regex
        +string value
        +evaluate(message: SMSMessage): boolean
    }
    
    class SMSMessage {
        +string id
        +string source
        +string destination
        +string content
        +Date submitTime
        +Date doneTime
        +string status
        +string errorCode
        +string userId
        +string connectorId
        +getDeliveryStatus(): string
        +getDuration(): number
    }
    
    class SMSStatistics {
        +collectUserStats(userId: string, timeRange: Object): Promise~Object~
        +collectSystemStats(timeRange: Object): Promise~Object~
        +getMessageVolume(params: Object): Promise~Object~
        +getDeliveryRates(params: Object): Promise~Object~
        +generateReport(params: Object): Promise~Buffer~
    }
    
    class JasminCLIAdapter {
        +string host
        +number port
        +string username
        +string password
        +connect(): Promise~boolean~
        +disconnect(): Promise~boolean~
        +executeCommand(command: string): Promise~string~
        +parseResponse(response: string): Object
    }
    
    class DashboardService {
        +getUserStats(userId: string): Promise~Object~
        +getSystemOverview(): Promise~Object~
        +getActiveConnectors(): Promise~SMPPConnector[]~
        +getRecentMessages(limit: number): Promise~SMSMessage[]~
        +getAlerts(): Promise~Object[]~
    }
    
    class AuthService {
        +login(username: string, password: string): Promise~Object~
        +logout(token: string): Promise~boolean~
        +validateToken(token: string): Promise~User~
        +refreshToken(token: string): Promise~string~
        +changePassword(userId: string, oldPassword: string, newPassword: string): Promise~boolean~
    }
    
    class HttpAPIClient {
        +string baseUrl
        +Object headers
        +sendSMS(params: Object): Promise~Object~
        +checkDeliveryStatus(messageId: string): Promise~string~
        +getBalance(userId: string): Promise~number~
        +executeRequest(path: string, method: string, data: Object): Promise~Object~
    }
    
    class ConfigurationManager {
        +getConfig(section: string): Promise~Object~
        +updateConfig(section: string, data: Object): Promise~boolean~
        +backupConfig(): Promise~string~
        +restoreConfig(backupId: string): Promise~boolean~
        +validateConfig(config: Object): Promise~Object~
    }
    
    class WebSocketManager {
        +initializeSocket(): void
        +subscribeToUpdates(channel: string, callback: function): string
        +unsubscribe(subscriptionId: string): void
        +publishUpdate(channel: string, data: Object): void
        +handleConnection(socket: Socket): void
        +handleDisconnection(socket: Socket): void
    }
    
    class APIController {
        +AuthService authService
        +DashboardService dashboardService
        +SMSStatistics statsService
        +ConfigurationManager configManager
        +handleRequest(req: Request, res: Response): void
        +authenticateRequest(req: Request): Promise~User~
        +authorizeAction(user: User, action: string): boolean
    }
    
    %% Relationships
    User "*" -- "*" Group : belongs to
    Route "1" -- "*" Filter : contains
    SMPPConnector -- SMSMessage : sends/receives
    User -- SMSMessage : owns
    JasminCLIAdapter -- SMPPConnector : manages
    JasminCLIAdapter -- Route : configures
    APIController -- AuthService : uses
    APIController -- DashboardService : uses
    APIController -- SMSStatistics : uses
    APIController -- ConfigurationManager : uses
    DashboardService -- SMSStatistics : uses
    WebSocketManager -- DashboardService : notifies
    HttpAPIClient -- SMSMessage : creates"
    }
  }
]
```

Now I'll create the sequence diagram to illustrate the program call flow for the Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway web interface.

```json
[
  {
    "command_name": "Editor.write",
    "args": {
      "path": "jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_sequence_diagram.mermaid",
      "content": "sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Web Client
    participant API as API Server
    participant Auth as AuthService
    participant Dashboard as DashboardService
    participant Stats as SMSStatistics
    participant CLI as JasminCLIAdapter
    participant HTTP as HttpAPIClient
    participant WS as WebSocketManager
    participant DB as Database
    participant Jasmin as Jasmin SMPP Gateway

    %% User Authentication Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/auth/login (credentials)
    API->>Auth: login(username, password)
    Auth->>DB: validateCredentials(username, password)
    DB-->>Auth: user object or error
    Auth-->>API: JWT token or error
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (token) or 401 Unauthorized

    %% Dashboard Loading Flow
    Client->>API: GET /api/dashboard (token)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user or error
    API->>Dashboard: getSystemOverview()
    Dashboard->>Stats: collectSystemStats(timeRange)
    Stats->>DB: queryStatistics(params)
    DB-->>Stats: statistics data
    Dashboard->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -l")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>Dashboard: parsed connector list
    Dashboard-->>API: compiled dashboard data
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (dashboard data)

    %% SMPP Connector Management Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/connectors (connector details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -a")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveConnectorDetails(connector)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error
    API->>WS: publishUpdate("connectors", { action: "created", data: connector })
    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (new connector)

    %% Start/Stop Connector Flow
    Client->>API: PUT /api/connectors/:id/status (action)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -1 {id} -S")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: updateConnectorStatus(id, status)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 200 OK or error
    API->>WS: publishUpdate("connectors", { action: "updated", data: connector })
    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (status change)

    %% Routing Management Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/routes (route details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("morouter -a")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveRouteDetails(route)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error

    %% SMS Sending Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/sms/send (message details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>HTTP: sendSMS(params)
    HTTP->>Jasmin: HTTP API request
    Jasmin-->>HTTP: message ID or error
    HTTP-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveMessageDetails(message)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 202 Accepted or error

    %% Real-time Statistics Update
    Jasmin->>Stats: message status update
    Stats->>DB: updateStatistics(data)
    Stats->>WS: publishUpdate("statistics", newData)
    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (new statistics)

    %% User Management Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/users (user details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated admin user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("user -a")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveUserDetails(user)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error

    %% Configuration Backup Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/config/backup
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated admin user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("persist")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveBackupRecord(backup)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (backup ID)

    %% Statistics Report Generation
    Client->>API: GET /api/reports/generate (parameters)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>Stats: generateReport(params)
    Stats->>DB: queryStatistics(params)
    DB-->>Stats: statistics data
    Stats-->>API: formatted report
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (report data/PDF)"
    }
  }
]
```

Now that I've read the PRD, I'll design a comprehensive system architecture for the Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway web interface. I'll focus on creating a design that meets all the requirements while ensuring the architecture is simple, maintainable, and leverages appropriate open-source libraries.

```json
[
  {
    "command_name": "Editor.write",
    "args": {
      "path": "jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_system_design.md",
      "content": "# Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway Web Interface: System Design

## Implementation Approach

Based on the PRD requirements, we need to design a web interface that provides complete access to Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway's CLI features while adding user-specific statistics tracking. The architecture must support real-time monitoring, comprehensive user management, and intuitive visualization of SMS traffic data.

### Key Technical Challenges

1. **CLI Command Integration**: We need to seamlessly translate web interface actions to Jasmin CLI commands and interpret their responses.
2. **Real-time Monitoring**: The system must provide real-time updates on SMPP connections, message queues, and system status.
3. **Statistics Collection and Storage**: We need an efficient way to collect, store, and query SMS statistics without impacting gateway performance.
4. **Authentication and Authorization**: The system requires secure, role-based access control that integrates with Jasmin's user management.
5. **Scalable Architecture**: The design must accommodate growing SMS traffic volumes and user base.

### Technology Selection

1. **Frontend**: 
   - React with TypeScript for type safety and enhanced development experience
   - Redux Toolkit for state management
   - Tailwind CSS for responsive UI
   - React Query for data fetching and caching
   - Chart.js and D3.js for data visualization
   - Socket.IO client for real-time updates

2. **Backend**:
   - Node.js with Express for the web server
   - Socket.IO for WebSocket connections
   - Passport.js for authentication
   - Prisma ORM for database interaction
   - Bull for job queuing and scheduling

3. **Database**:
   - PostgreSQL for relational data (users, configurations)
   - TimescaleDB extension for time-series data (statistics)

4. **Jasmin Integration**:
   - Custom adapter for Jasmin's Management PB API
   - HTTP client for Jasmin's HTTP API
   - Custom telnet client for CLI command execution

5. **Deployment**:
   - Docker and Docker Compose for containerization
   - GitHub Actions for CI/CD
   - Nginx as reverse proxy

## Data Structures and Interfaces

The system architecture consists of several key components organized in a layered approach:

1. **Presentation Layer**: React frontend application
2. **API Layer**: Express.js REST API
3. **Service Layer**: Core business logic and integrations
4. **Data Layer**: Databases and data access
5. **Integration Layer**: Jasmin SMPP Gateway interaction

Each component is designed with clear interfaces and responsibilities, following clean architecture principles to ensure separation of concerns and testability.

## System Components

### 1. Frontend Application

The React-based frontend provides an intuitive interface for all Jasmin SMPP Gateway management tasks. Key features include:

- Responsive dashboard with live statistics
- User and group management forms
- Connector configuration interfaces
- Visual route builder
- Interactive charts for SMS traffic analysis

### 2. Backend API Server

The Express.js backend serves as the central coordination point, handling:

- REST API endpoints for frontend requests
- Authentication and authorization
- WebSocket connections for real-time updates
- Request validation and error handling
- Statistics aggregation and reporting

### 3. Jasmin Integration Service

This service handles all interactions with the Jasmin SMPP Gateway:

- CLI command execution via telnet
- Management PB API integration
- HTTP API client for message operations
- Connection monitoring
- Configuration management

### 4. Statistics Collection Service

Responsible for gathering, processing, and storing SMS traffic statistics:

- Message throughput tracking
- User-specific statistics collection
- Delivery status monitoring
- Performance metrics aggregation
- Report generation

### 5. Database Systems

The data storage layer consists of:

- PostgreSQL for user data, configurations, and system settings
- TimescaleDB for time-series statistics data
- Redis for caching and session management

## Integration with Jasmin SMPP Gateway

The system integrates with Jasmin SMPP Gateway through multiple channels:

1. **Telnet Interface**: For executing CLI commands and receiving responses
2. **HTTP API**: For sending/receiving SMS messages and simple operations
3. **Management PB API**: For advanced management operations
4. **Log Files**: For collecting additional diagnostic information
5. **Custom Metrics Collection**: For gathering detailed statistics

## Authentication and Authorization

The system implements a comprehensive security model:

1. **User Authentication**: JWT-based authentication with secure password handling
2. **Role-Based Access Control**: Admin, Manager, and User roles with appropriate permissions
3. **Session Management**: Secure session handling with appropriate timeouts
4. **API Security**: HTTPS enforcement, rate limiting, and input validation

## Scalability Considerations

The architecture is designed to scale horizontally:

1. **Stateless API Servers**: Multiple instances can operate behind a load balancer
2. **Separate Statistics Processing**: Background workers handle statistics collection
3. **Database Optimization**: Indexes and partitioning for efficient queries
4. **Caching Strategy**: Redis-based caching for frequently accessed data

## Deployment Architecture

The system is containerized using Docker for easy deployment and scaling:

1. **Frontend Container**: Serves the React application
2. **API Server Container**: Handles REST API requests
3. **Worker Container**: Processes background jobs and statistics collection
4. **Database Containers**: PostgreSQL and Redis
5. **Nginx Container**: Acts as reverse proxy and static file server

## Monitoring and Logging

Comprehensive monitoring ensures system health and performance:

1. **Application Logging**: Structured logs with appropriate log levels
2. **Performance Metrics**: CPU, memory, and response time tracking
3. **Error Tracking**: Centralized error collection and notification
4. **Audit Trail**: Tracking of administrative actions

## Anything UNCLEAR

1. **Jasmin Version Compatibility**: The design assumes compatibility with recent Jasmin SMPP Gateway versions. Specific version requirements should be clarified.
2. **Performance Requirements**: Specific throughput expectations would help refine the architecture for scale.
3. **Data Retention Policies**: Clarification on how long statistics data should be retained would guide database design decisions.
4. **Integration with External Systems**: Requirements for integrating with external billing or monitoring systems should be explored further.
5. **Deployment Environment**: Details about the target deployment environment would help optimize resource allocation and configuration."
    }
  }
]
```

```json
    class User {
        +string id
        +string username
        +string passwordHash
        +string email
        +string[] roles
        +boolean isActive
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
        +authenticate(password: string): boolean
        +hasPermission(permission: string): boolean
    }
    
    class Group {
        +string id
        +string name
        +string description
        +User[] members
        +Object permissions
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
        +addUser(user: User): void
        +removeUser(user: User): void
        +hasPermission(permission: string): boolean
    }
    
    class SMPPConnector {
        +string id
        +string name
        +string host
        +number port
        +string systemId
        +string password
        +string systemType
        +ConnectionStatus status
        +Object advancedParams
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
        +connect(): Promise~boolean~
        +disconnect(): Promise~boolean~
        +getStatus(): ConnectionStatus
        +updateConfig(params: Object): Promise~boolean~
    }
    
    class Route {
        +string id
        +string type %% MO or MT
        +string connectorId
        +Filter[] filters
        +number priority
        +Date createdAt
        +Date updatedAt
        +matchesMessage(message: SMSMessage): boolean
        +applyRoute(message: SMSMessage): Promise~boolean~
    }
    
    class Filter {
        +string id
        +string type %% e.g., destination_number, source_number, content
        +string operator %% e.g., equal, contains, regex
        +string value
        +evaluate(message: SMSMessage): boolean
    }
    
    class SMSMessage {
        +string id
        +string source
        +string destination
        +string content
        +Date submitTime
        +Date doneTime
        +string status
        +string errorCode
        +string userId
        +string connectorId
        +getDeliveryStatus(): string
        +getDuration(): number
    }
    
    class SMSStatistics {
        +collectUserStats(userId: string, timeRange: Object): Promise~Object~
        +collectSystemStats(timeRange: Object): Promise~Object~
        +getMessageVolume(params: Object): Promise~Object~
        +getDeliveryRates(params: Object): Promise~Object~
        +generateReport(params: Object): Promise~Buffer~
    }
    
    class JasminCLIAdapter {
        +string host
        +number port
        +string username
        +string password
        +connect(): Promise~boolean~
        +disconnect(): Promise~boolean~
        +executeCommand(command: string): Promise~string~
        +parseResponse(response: string): Object
    }
    
    class DashboardService {
        +getUserStats(userId: string): Promise~Object~
        +getSystemOverview(): Promise~Object~
        +getActiveConnectors(): Promise~SMPPConnector[]~
        +getRecentMessages(limit: number): Promise~SMSMessage[]~
        +getAlerts(): Promise~Object[]~
    }
    
    class AuthService {
        +login(username: string, password: string): Promise~Object~
        +logout(token: string): Promise~boolean~
        +validateToken(token: string): Promise~User~
        +refreshToken(token: string): Promise~string~
        +changePassword(userId: string, oldPassword: string, newPassword: string): Promise~boolean~
    }
    
    class HttpAPIClient {
        +string baseUrl
        +Object headers
        +sendSMS(params: Object): Promise~Object~
        +checkDeliveryStatus(messageId: string): Promise~string~
        +getBalance(userId: string): Promise~number~
        +executeRequest(path: string, method: string, data: Object): Promise~Object~
    }
    
    class ConfigurationManager {
        +getConfig(section: string): Promise~Object~
        +updateConfig(section: string, data: Object): Promise~boolean~
        +backupConfig(): Promise~string~
        +restoreConfig(backupId: string): Promise~boolean~
        +validateConfig(config: Object): Promise~Object~
    }
    
    class WebSocketManager {
        +initializeSocket(): void
        +subscribeToUpdates(channel: string, callback: function): string
        +unsubscribe(subscriptionId: string): void
        +publishUpdate(channel: string, data: Object): void
        +handleConnection(socket: Socket): void
        +handleDisconnection(socket: Socket): void
    }
    
    class APIController {
        +AuthService authService
        +DashboardService dashboardService
        +SMSStatistics statsService
        +ConfigurationManager configManager
        +handleRequest(req: Request, res: Response): void
        +authenticateRequest(req: Request): Promise~User~
        +authorizeAction(user: User, action: string): boolean
    }
    
    %% Relationships
    User "*" -- "*" Group : belongs to
    Route "1" -- "*" Filter : contains
    SMPPConnector -- SMSMessage : sends/receives
    User -- SMSMessage : owns
    JasminCLIAdapter -- SMPPConnector : manages
    JasminCLIAdapter -- Route : configures
    APIController -- AuthService : uses
    APIController -- DashboardService : uses
    APIController -- SMSStatistics : uses
    APIController -- ConfigurationManager : uses
    DashboardService -- SMSStatistics : uses
    WebSocketManager -- DashboardService : notifies
    HttpAPIClient -- SMSMessage : creates"
    }
  }
]
```

Now I'll create the sequence diagram to illustrate the program call flow for the Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway web interface.

```json
[
  {
    "command_name": "Editor.write",
    "args": {
      "path": "jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_sequence_diagram.mermaid",
      "content": "sequenceDiagram
    participant Client as Web Client
    participant API as API Server
    participant Auth as AuthService
    participant Dashboard as DashboardService
    participant Stats as SMSStatistics
    participant CLI as JasminCLIAdapter
    participant HTTP as HttpAPIClient
    participant WS as WebSocketManager
    participant DB as Database
    participant Jasmin as Jasmin SMPP Gateway

    %% User Authentication Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/auth/login (credentials)
    API->>Auth: login(username, password)
    Auth->>DB: validateCredentials(username, password)
    DB-->>Auth: user object or error
    Auth-->>API: JWT token or error
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (token) or 401 Unauthorized

    %% Dashboard Loading Flow
    Client->>API: GET /api/dashboard (token)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user or error
    API->>Dashboard: getSystemOverview()
    Dashboard->>Stats: collectSystemStats(timeRange)
    Stats->>DB: queryStatistics(params)
    DB-->>Stats: statistics data
    Dashboard->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -l")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>Dashboard: parsed connector list
    Dashboard-->>API: compiled dashboard data
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (dashboard data)

    %% SMPP Connector Management Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/connectors (connector details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -a")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveConnectorDetails(connector)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error
    API->>WS: publishUpdate("connectors", { action: "created", data: connector })
    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (new connector)

    %% Start/Stop Connector Flow
    Client->>API: PUT /api/connectors/:id/status (action)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -1 {id} -S")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: updateConnectorStatus(id, status)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 200 OK or error
    API->>WS: publishUpdate("connectors", { action: "updated", data: connector })
    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (status change)

    %% Routing Management Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/routes (route details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("morouter -a")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveRouteDetails(route)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error

    %% SMS Sending Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/sms/send (message details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>HTTP: sendSMS(params)
    HTTP->>Jasmin: HTTP API request
    Jasmin-->>HTTP: message ID or error
    HTTP-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveMessageDetails(message)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 202 Accepted or error

    %% Real-time Statistics Update
    Jasmin->>Stats: message status update
    Stats->>DB: updateStatistics(data)
    Stats->>WS: publishUpdate("statistics", newData)
    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (new statistics)

    %% User Management Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/users (user details)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated admin user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("user -a")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveUserDetails(user)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error

    %% Configuration Backup Flow
    Client->>API: POST /api/config/backup
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated admin user
    API->>CLI: executeCommand("persist")
    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
    CLI-->>API: operation result
    API->>DB: saveBackupRecord(backup)
    DB-->>API: operation result
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (backup ID)

    %% Statistics Report Generation
    Client->>API: GET /api/reports/generate (parameters)
    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
    Auth-->>API: validated user
    API->>Stats: generateReport(params)
    Stats->>DB: queryStatistics(params)
    DB-->>Stats: statistics data
    Stats-->>API: formatted report
    API-->>Client: 200 OK (report data/PDF)"
    }
  }
]
```

Bob.instruction:
You are an architect. Your task is to design a software system that meets the requirements.
1. If Product Requirement Document (PRD) is provided, read it first with Editor.read in a single response without any other commands. After reading, use it as the requirement.
2. For web app or game design:

m on top of a template. See the Template section for more details.

3. You should output a system design that includes the following sections: 
 - Implementation approach: Analyze the difficult points of the requirements, select the appropriate open-source framework.
 - Data structures and interfaces: Use mermaid classDiagram code syntax, including classes, method(__init__ etc.) and functions with type annotations, CLEARLY MARK the RELATIONSHIPS between classes, and comply with PEP8 standards. The data structures SHOULD BE VERY DETAILED and the API should be comprehensive with a complete design.
 - Program call flow: Use sequenceDiagram code syntax, COMPLETE and VERY DETAILED, using CLASSES AND API DEFINED ABOVE accurately, covering the CRUD AND INIT of each object, SYNTAX MUST BE CORRECT.
 - Anything UNCLEAR: Mention unclear project aspects, then try to clarify it.
4. Use Editor.write to write the system design in markdown format. The file path should be "{project_name}_system_design.md". Use command_name "end" when the system design is finished.
5. If not mentioned, always use Editor.write to write "Program call flow" in a new file name "{project_name}_sequence_diagram.mermaid" and write "Data structures and interfaces" in a new file "{project_name}_class_diagram.mermaid". Mermaid code only. Do not add "```mermaid".

## System Design Example

```markdown
## Implementation approach": 
We will ...

## Data structures and interfaces:
classDiagram
    class Main {
        <<entry point>>
        +main() str
    }
    class SearchEngine {
        +search(query: str) str
    }
    class Index {
        +create_index(data: dict)
        +query_index(query: str) list
    }
    class Ranking {
        +rank_results(results: list) list
}

## Program call flow:
sequenceDiagram
    participant M as Main
    participant SE as SearchEngine
    participant I as Index
    participant R as Ranking
    participant S as Summary
    participant KB as KnowledgeBase
    M->>SE: search(query)
    SE->>I: query_index(query)
    I->>KB: fetch_data(query)
    KB-->>I: return data


## Anything UNCLEAR
Clarification needed on third-party API integration, ...
```

schemas.description: A tool for reading, understanding, writing, and editing files. Support local file including text-based files (txt, md, json, py, html, js, css, etc.), pdf, docx, excluding images, csv, excel, or online links For agent: Do NOT initiate multiple Editor.insert_content_at_line calls at the same time, since the line number will change starting with the first execution, making line number of the subsequent calls incorrect. Split the calls into separate responses. For the same reason, Editor.insert_content_at_line should NOT go behind Editor.edit_file_by_replace in the same response. Perform insert operation in a separate response.

write.description: Write the whole content to a file. When used, make sure content arg contains the full content of the file.

read.description: Read the whole content of a file. Using an absolute path as the argument for specifying the file location.

similarity_search.description: Given a filename or a pathname, performs a similarity search for a given query across the specified file or path. This method searches the index repository for the provided query, classifying the specified files or paths. It performs a search on each cluster of files and handles non-indexed files separately, merging results from structured indices with any direct results from non-indexed files. This function call does not depend on other functions.
similarity_search.parameters: Args: query (str): The search query string to look for in the indexed files. path (Union[str, Path]): A pathname or filename to search within. Returns: List[str]: A list of results as strings, containing the text from the merged results and any direct results from non-indexed files. Example: >>> query = "The problem to be analyzed from the document" >>> file_or_path = "The pathname or filename you want to search within" >>> texts: List[str] = await Editor.similarity_search(query=query, path=file_or_path) >>> print(texts)

schemas.description: A role who can think and act dynamically

ask_human.description: Use this when you fail the current task or if you are unsure of the situation encountered. Your response should contain a brief summary of your situation, ended with a clear and concise question.

reply_to_human.description: Reply to human user with the content provided. Use this when you have a clear answer or solution to the user's question.

Command Editor.read executed: path='/data/chats/015rz/workspace/../workspace/jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_system_design.md' content="001|# Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway Web Interface: System Design
002|
003|## Implementation Approach
004|
005|Based on the PRD requirements, we need to design a web interface that provides complete access to Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway's CLI features while adding user-specific statistics tracking. The architecture must support real-time monitoring, comprehensive user management, and intuitive visualization of SMS traffic data.
006|
007|### Key Technical Challenges
008|
009|1. **CLI Command Integration**: We need to seamlessly translate web interface actions to Jasmin CLI commands and interpret their responses.
010|2. **Real-time Monitoring**: The system must provide real-time updates on SMPP connections, message queues, and system status.
011|3. **Statistics Collection and Storage**: We need an efficient way to collect, store, and query SMS statistics without impacting gateway performance.
012|4. **Authentication and Authorization**: The system requires secure, role-based access control that integrates with Jasmin's user management.
013|5. **Scalable Architecture**: The design must accommodate growing SMS traffic volumes and user base.
014|
015|### Technology Selection
016|
017|1. **Frontend**: 
018|   - React with TypeScript for type safety and enhanced development experience
019|   - Redux Toolkit for state management
020|   - Tailwind CSS for responsive UI
021|   - React Query for data fetching and caching
022|   - Chart.js and D3.js for data visualization
023|   - Socket.IO client for real-time updates
024|
025|2. **Backend**:
026|   - Node.js with Express for the web server
027|   - Socket.IO for WebSocket connections
028|   - Passport.js for authentication
029|   - Prisma ORM for database interaction
030|   - Bull for job queuing and scheduling
031|
032|3. **Database**:
033|   - PostgreSQL for relational data (users, configurations)
034|   - TimescaleDB extension for time-series data (statistics)
035|
036|4. **Jasmin Integration**:
037|   - Custom adapter for Jasmin's Management PB API
038|   - HTTP client for Jasmin's HTTP API
039|   - Custom telnet client for CLI command execution
040|
041|5. **Deployment**:
042|   - Docker and Docker Compose for containerization
043|   - GitHub Actions for CI/CD
044|   - Nginx as reverse proxy
045|
046|## Data Structures and Interfaces
047|
048|The system architecture consists of several key components organized in a layered approach:
049|
050|1. **Presentation Layer**: React frontend application
051|2. **API Layer**: Express.js REST API
052|3. **Service Layer**: Core business logic and integrations
053|4. **Data Layer**: Databases and data access
054|5. **Integration Layer**: Jasmin SMPP Gateway interaction
055|
056|Each component is designed with clear interfaces and responsibilities, following clean architecture principles to ensure separation of concerns and testability.
057|
058|## System Components
059|
060|### 1. Frontend Application
061|
062|The React-based frontend provides an intuitive interface for all Jasmin SMPP Gateway management tasks. Key features include:
063|
064|- Responsive dashboard with live statistics
065|- User and group management forms
066|- Connector configuration interfaces
067|- Visual route builder
068|- Interactive charts for SMS traffic analysis
069|
070|### 2. Backend API Server
071|
072|The Express.js backend serves as the central coordination point, handling:
073|
074|- REST API endpoints for frontend requests
075|- Authentication and authorization
076|- WebSocket connections for real-time updates
077|- Request validation and error handling
078|- Statistics aggregation and reporting
079|
080|### 3. Jasmin Integration Service
081|
082|This service handles all interactions with the Jasmin SMPP Gateway:
083|
084|- CLI command execution via telnet
085|- Management PB API integration
086|- HTTP API client for message operations
087|- Connection monitoring
088|- Configuration management
089|
090|### 4. Statistics Collection Service
091|
092|Responsible for gathering, processing, and storing SMS traffic statistics:
093|
094|- Message throughput tracking
095|- User-specific statistics collection
096|- Delivery status monitoring
097|- Performance metrics aggregation
098|- Report generation
099|
100|### 5. Database Systems
101|
102|The data storage layer consists of:
103|
104|- PostgreSQL for user data, configurations, and system settings
105|- TimescaleDB for time-series statistics data
106|- Redis for caching and session management
107|
108|## Integration with Jasmin SMPP Gateway
109|
110|The system integrates with Jasmin SMPP Gateway through multiple channels:
111|
112|1. **Telnet Interface**: For executing CLI commands and receiving responses
113|2. **HTTP API**: For sending/receiving SMS messages and simple operations
114|3. **Management PB API**: For advanced management operations
115|4. **Log Files**: For collecting additional diagnostic information
116|5. **Custom Metrics Collection**: For gathering detailed statistics
117|
118|## Authentication and Authorization
119|
120|The system implements a comprehensive security model:
121|
122|1. **User Authentication**: JWT-based authentication with secure password handling
123|2. **Role-Based Access Control**: Admin, Manager, and User roles with appropriate permissions
124|3. **Session Management**: Secure session handling with appropriate timeouts
125|4. **API Security**: HTTPS enforcement, rate limiting, and input validation
126|
127|## Scalability Considerations
128|
129|The architecture is designed to scale horizontally:
130|
131|1. **Stateless API Servers**: Multiple instances can operate behind a load balancer
132|2. **Separate Statistics Processing**: Background workers handle statistics collection
133|3. **Database Optimization**: Indexes and partitioning for efficient queries
134|4. **Caching Strategy**: Redis-based caching for frequently accessed data
135|
136|## Deployment Architecture
137|
138|The system is containerized using Docker for easy deployment and scaling:
139|
140|1. **Frontend Container**: Serves the React application
141|2. **API Server Container**: Handles REST API requests
142|3. **Worker Container**: Processes background jobs and statistics collection
143|4. **Database Containers**: PostgreSQL and Redis
144|5. **Nginx Container**: Acts as reverse proxy and static file server
145|
146|## Monitoring and Logging
147|
148|Comprehensive monitoring ensures system health and performance:
149|
150|1. **Application Logging**: Structured logs with appropriate log levels
151|2. **Performance Metrics**: CPU, memory, and response time tracking
152|3. **Error Tracking**: Centralized error collection and notification
153|4. **Audit Trail**: Tracking of administrative actions
154|
155|## Anything UNCLEAR
156|
157|1. **Jasmin Version Compatibility**: The design assumes compatibility with recent Jasmin SMPP Gateway versions. Specific version requirements should be clarified.
158|2. **Performance Requirements**: Specific throughput expectations would help refine the architecture for scale.
159|3. **Data Retention Policies**: Clarification on how long statistics data should be retained would guide database design decisions.
160|4. **Integration with External Systems**: Requirements for integrating with external billing or monitoring systems should be explored further.
161|5. **Deployment Environment**: Details about the target deployment environment would help optimize resource allocation and configuration."

By combining all CLI features with intuitive web-based management tools and detailed user-specific analytics, this product will significantly enhance the usability and value of the Jasmin SMS Gateway platform. 285| 286|The successful implementation of this project will enable administrators to efficiently manage the SMS gateway through a modern web interface, allow business owners to track usage for billing purposes, and provide operations teams with the tools needed for effective monitoring and troubleshooting.'

Command Editor.read executed: path='/data/chats/015rz/workspace/../workspace/jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_system_design.md' content="001|# Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway Web Interface: System Design
002|
003|## Implementation Approach
004|
005|Based on the PRD requirements, we need to design a web interface that provides complete access to Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway's CLI features while adding user-specific statistics tracking. The architecture must support real-time monitoring, comprehensive user management, and intuitive visualization of SMS traffic data.
006|
007|### Key Technical Challenges
008|
009|1. **CLI Command Integration**: We need to seamlessly translate web interface actions to Jasmin CLI commands and interpret their responses.
010|2. **Real-time Monitoring**: The system must provide real-time updates on SMPP connections, message queues, and system status.
011|3. **Statistics Collection and Storage**: We need an efficient way to collect, store, and query SMS statistics without impacting gateway performance.
012|4. **Authentication and Authorization**: The system requires secure, role-based access control that integrates with Jasmin's user management.
013|5. **Scalable Architecture**: The design must accommodate growing SMS traffic volumes and user base.
014|
015|### Technology Selection
016|
017|1. **Frontend**: 
018|   - React with TypeScript for type safety and enhanced development experience
019|   - Redux Toolkit for state management
020|   - Tailwind CSS for responsive UI
021|   - React Query for data fetching and caching
022|   - Chart.js and D3.js for data visualization
023|   - Socket.IO client for real-time updates
024|
025|2. **Backend**:
026|   - Node.js with Express for the web server
027|   - Socket.IO for WebSocket connections
028|   - Passport.js for authentication
029|   - Prisma ORM for database interaction
030|   - Bull for job queuing and scheduling
031|
032|3. **Database**:
033|   - PostgreSQL for relational data (users, configurations)
034|   - TimescaleDB extension for time-series data (statistics)
035|
036|4. **Jasmin Integration**:
037|   - Custom adapter for Jasmin's Management PB API
038|   - HTTP client for Jasmin's HTTP API
039|   - Custom telnet client for CLI command execution
040|
041|5. **Deployment**:
042|   - Docker and Docker Compose for containerization
043|   - GitHub Actions for CI/CD
044|   - Nginx as reverse proxy
045|
046|## Data Structures and Interfaces
047|
048|The system architecture consists of several key components organized in a layered approach:
049|
050|1. **Presentation Layer**: React frontend application
051|2. **API Layer**: Express.js REST API
052|3. **Service Layer**: Core business logic and integrations
053|4. **Data Layer**: Databases and data access
054|5. **Integration Layer**: Jasmin SMPP Gateway interaction
055|
056|Each component is designed with clear interfaces and responsibilities, following clean architecture principles to ensure separation of concerns and testability.
057|
058|## System Components
059|
060|### 1. Frontend Application
061|
062|The React-based frontend provides an intuitive interface for all Jasmin SMPP Gateway management tasks. Key features include:
063|
064|- Responsive dashboard with live statistics
065|- User and group management forms
066|- Connector configuration interfaces
067|- Visual route builder
068|- Interactive charts for SMS traffic analysis
069|
070|### 2. Backend API Server
071|
072|The Express.js backend serves as the central coordination point, handling:
073|
074|- REST API endpoints for frontend requests
075|- Authentication and authorization
076|- WebSocket connections for real-time updates
077|- Request validation and error handling
078|- Statistics aggregation and reporting
079|
080|### 3. Jasmin Integration Service
081|
082|This service handles all interactions with the Jasmin SMPP Gateway:
083|
084|- CLI command execution via telnet
085|- Management PB API integration
086|- HTTP API client for message operations
087|- Connection monitoring
088|- Configuration management
089|
090|### 4. Statistics Collection Service
091|
092|Responsible for gathering, processing, and storing SMS traffic statistics:
093|
094|- Message throughput tracking
095|- User-specific statistics collection
096|- Delivery status monitoring
097|- Performance metrics aggregation
098|- Report generation
099|
100|### 5. Database Systems
101|
102|The data storage layer consists of:
103|
104|- PostgreSQL for user data, configurations, and system settings
105|- TimescaleDB for time-series statistics data
106|- Redis for caching and session management
107|
108|## Integration with Jasmin SMPP Gateway
109|
110|The system integrates with Jasmin SMPP Gateway through multiple channels:
111|
112|1. **Telnet Interface**: For executing CLI commands and receiving responses
113|2. **HTTP API**: For sending/receiving SMS messages and simple operations
114|3. **Management PB API**: For advanced management operations
115|4. **Log Files**: For collecting additional diagnostic information
116|5. **Custom Metrics Collection**: For gathering detailed statistics
117|
118|## Authentication and Authorization
119|
120|The system implements a comprehensive security model:
121|
122|1. **User Authentication**: JWT-based authentication with secure password handling
123|2. **Role-Based Access Control**: Admin, Manager, and User roles with appropriate permissions
124|3. **Session Management**: Secure session handling with appropriate timeouts
125|4. **API Security**: HTTPS enforcement, rate limiting, and input validation
126|
127|## Scalability Considerations
128|
129|The architecture is designed to scale horizontally:
130|
131|1. **Stateless API Servers**: Multiple instances can operate behind a load balancer
132|2. **Separate Statistics Processing**: Background workers handle statistics collection
133|3. **Database Optimization**: Indexes and partitioning for efficient queries
134|4. **Caching Strategy**: Redis-based caching for frequently accessed data
135|
136|## Deployment Architecture
137|
138|The system is containerized using Docker for easy deployment and scaling:
139|
140|1. **Frontend Container**: Serves the React application
141|2. **API Server Container**: Handles REST API requests
142|3. **Worker Container**: Processes background jobs and statistics collection
143|4. **Database Containers**: PostgreSQL and Redis
144|5. **Nginx Container**: Acts as reverse proxy and static file server
145|
146|## Monitoring and Logging
147|
148|Comprehensive monitoring ensures system health and performance:
149|
150|1. **Application Logging**: Structured logs with appropriate log levels
151|2. **Performance Metrics**: CPU, memory, and response time tracking
152|3. **Error Tracking**: Centralized error collection and notification
153|4. **Audit Trail**: Tracking of administrative actions
154|
155|## Anything UNCLEAR
156|
157|1. **Jasmin Version Compatibility**: The design assumes compatibility with recent Jasmin SMPP Gateway versions. Specific version requirements should be clarified.
158|2. **Performance Requirements**: Specific throughput expectations would help refine the architecture for scale.
159|3. **Data Retention Policies**: Clarification on how long statistics data should be retained would guide database design decisions.
160|4. **Integration with External Systems**: Requirements for integrating with external billing or monitoring systems should be explored further.
161|5. **Deployment Environment**: Details about the target deployment environment would help optimize resource allocation and configuration."

Command Editor.read executed: path='/data/chats/015rz/workspace/../workspace/jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_sequence_diagram.mermaid' content='001|sequenceDiagram
002|    participant Client as Web Client
003|    participant API as API Server
004|    participant Auth as AuthService
005|    participant Dashboard as DashboardService
006|    participant Stats as SMSStatistics
007|    participant CLI as JasminCLIAdapter
008|    participant HTTP as HttpAPIClient
009|    participant WS as WebSocketManager
010|    participant DB as Database
011|    participant Jasmin as Jasmin SMPP Gateway
012|
013|    %% User Authentication Flow
014|    Client->>API: POST /api/auth/login (credentials)
015|    API->>Auth: login(username, password)
016|    Auth->>DB: validateCredentials(username, password)
017|    DB-->>Auth: user object or error
018|    Auth-->>API: JWT token or error
019|    API-->>Client: 200 OK (token) or 401 Unauthorized
020|
021|    %% Dashboard Loading Flow
022|    Client->>API: GET /api/dashboard (token)
023|    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
024|    Auth-->>API: validated user or error
025|    API->>Dashboard: getSystemOverview()
026|    Dashboard->>Stats: collectSystemStats(timeRange)
027|    Stats->>DB: queryStatistics(params)
028|    DB-->>Stats: statistics data
029|    Dashboard->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -l")
030|    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
031|    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
032|    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
033|    CLI-->>Dashboard: parsed connector list
034|    Dashboard-->>API: compiled dashboard data
035|    API-->>Client: 200 OK (dashboard data)
036|
037|    %% SMPP Connector Management Flow
038|    Client->>API: POST /api/connectors (connector details)
039|    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
040|    Auth-->>API: validated user
041|    API->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -a")
042|    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
043|    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
044|    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
045|    CLI-->>API: operation result
046|    API->>DB: saveConnectorDetails(connector)
047|    DB-->>API: operation result
048|    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error
049|    API->>WS: publishUpdate("connectors", { action: "created", data: connector })
050|    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (new connector)
051|
052|    %% Start/Stop Connector Flow
053|    Client->>API: PUT /api/connectors/:id/status (action)
054|    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
055|    Auth-->>API: validated user
056|    API->>CLI: executeCommand("connector -1 {id} -S")
057|    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
058|    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
059|    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
060|    CLI-->>API: operation result
061|    API->>DB: updateConnectorStatus(id, status)
062|    DB-->>API: operation result
063|    API-->>Client: 200 OK or error
064|    API->>WS: publishUpdate("connectors", { action: "updated", data: connector })
065|    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (status change)
066|
067|    %% Routing Management Flow
068|    Client->>API: POST /api/routes (route details)
069|    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
070|    Auth-->>API: validated user
071|    API->>CLI: executeCommand("morouter -a")
072|    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
073|    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
074|    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
075|    CLI-->>API: operation result
076|    API->>DB: saveRouteDetails(route)
077|    DB-->>API: operation result
078|    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error
079|
080|    %% SMS Sending Flow
081|    Client->>API: POST /api/sms/send (message details)
082|    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
083|    Auth-->>API: validated user
084|    API->>HTTP: sendSMS(params)
085|    HTTP->>Jasmin: HTTP API request
086|    Jasmin-->>HTTP: message ID or error
087|    HTTP-->>API: operation result
088|    API->>DB: saveMessageDetails(message)
089|    DB-->>API: operation result
090|    API-->>Client: 202 Accepted or error
091|
092|    %% Real-time Statistics Update
093|    Jasmin->>Stats: message status update
094|    Stats->>DB: updateStatistics(data)
095|    Stats->>WS: publishUpdate("statistics", newData)
096|    WS-->>Client: WebSocket update (new statistics)
097|
098|    %% User Management Flow
099|    Client->>API: POST /api/users (user details)
100|    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
101|    Auth-->>API: validated admin user
102|    API->>CLI: executeCommand("user -a")
103|    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
104|    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
105|    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
106|    CLI-->>API: operation result
107|    API->>DB: saveUserDetails(user)
108|    DB-->>API: operation result
109|    API-->>Client: 201 Created or error
110|
111|    %% Configuration Backup Flow
112|    Client->>API: POST /api/config/backup
113|    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
114|    Auth-->>API: validated admin user
115|    API->>CLI: executeCommand("persist")
116|    CLI->>Jasmin: connect()
117|    CLI->>Jasmin: send command
118|    Jasmin-->>CLI: command response
119|    CLI-->>API: operation result
120|    API->>DB: saveBackupRecord(backup)
121|    DB-->>API: operation result
122|    API-->>Client: 200 OK (backup ID)
123|
124|    %% Statistics Report Generation
125|    Client->>API: GET /api/reports/generate (parameters)
126|    API->>Auth: validateToken(token)
127|    Auth-->>API: validated user
128|    API->>Stats: generateReport(params)
129|    Stats->>DB: queryStatistics(params)
130|    DB-->>Stats: statistics data
131|    Stats-->>API: formatted report
132|    API-->>Client: 200 OK (report data/PDF)'

prev_summary.content:
Previous conversation summary: ## Total Summary
The team developed and deployed a web application for the Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway, enhancing routing, accessibility, error handling, and authentication features.

## User Requirement
The user required a fully functional web application for managing the Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway, including CLI integration and SMS statistics tracking; implementation is complete, and the API service and routing improvements were ongoing.

## Files
- **User-Provided Files:**
  - `jasmin_smpp_gateway_web_interface_prd.md`: Product Requirements Document detailing project goals, user stories, and technical specifications.
  - `jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_system_design.md`: System design document outlining architecture and integration points.
  - `jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_class_diagram.mermaid`: Class diagram representing data structures and relationships.
  - `jasmin_smpp_web_dashboard_sequence_diagram.mermaid`: Sequence diagram illustrating program flow for key operations.

- **Files Written/Edited:**
  - `index.html`: Updated title to "Jasmin SMPP SMS Gateway Dashboard".
  - `src/App.jsx`: Main application structure with improved routing, error handling, and loading states.
  - `src/components/layout/Sidebar.jsx`: Sidebar component for navigation.
  - `src/components/layout/Header.jsx`: Header component with notifications and user menu.
  - `src/components/layout/Footer.jsx`: Footer component with links.
  - `src/contexts/AuthContext.jsx`: Authentication context updated for error handling, token validation, and session management.
  - `src/hooks/useAuth.js`: Custom hook for accessing authentication context.
  - `src/services/api.js`: API service for handling requests to the backend with enhanced error handling and token management.
  - `src/pages/Dashboard.jsx`: Dashboard page displaying statistics and metrics.
  - `src/pages/Login.jsx`: Login page for user authentication.
  - `src/pages/NotFound.jsx`: 404 Not Found page.
  - `src/pages/Users.jsx`: User management page with user list and actions.
  - `src/pages/Groups.jsx`: Group management page with group list and actions.
  - `src/pages/Connectors.jsx`: Connectors management page with connector list and actions.
  - `src/pages/Routes.jsx`: Routes management page with route list and actions.
  - `src/pages/Statistics.jsx`: Statistics page for displaying SMS statistics.
  - `src/components/forms/ConnectorForm.jsx`: Form component for adding/editing connectors.
  - `src/components/forms/UserForm.jsx`: Form component for adding/editing users.
  - `src/store/index.js`: Redux store configuration.
  - `src/store/authSlice.js`: Redux slice for authentication state management.
  - `src/store/connectorSlice.js`: Redux slice for managing connectors.
  - `src/utils/constants.js`: Constants used throughout the application.
  - `src/components/common/StatusBadge.jsx`: Component for displaying status badges.
  - `src/components/common/LoadingSpinner.jsx`: Loading spinner component.
  - `src/components/common/Card.jsx`: Card component for displaying content.
  - `src/components/dashboard/StatCard.jsx`: Card component for displaying statistics.
  - `src/components/dashboard/ConnectorStatus.jsx`: Component for displaying connector statuses.

## Completed Tasks
The application is now accessible at: https://jasmin-smpp-dashboard-ni4287-v1.mgx.world. Key tasks included enhancing error handling, authentication, and routing, followed by a successful deployment.

{current_state}

Alex.instruction:
You are a world-class engineer, your goal is to write google-style, elegant, modular, readable, maintainable, fully functional, and ready-for-production code. You have been tasked with developing a web app or game. Unless the user or a system design specifies, or an existing repo is provided, you should use a React template with Tailwind CSS and JavaScript. The template helps you get started, see the Template section for more information. 0. Principles - Outline all features to develop based on the user requirements. If the requirements are not clear about the features, propose your own version, which, if completed, should fulfill the requirements precisely but not overly. - During your development, ensure NO omission NOR surplus of your listed features. NO MORE, NO LESS. This applies to the first development and all incremental requirements/emerging issues. - DON'T make improvements without user's consent. End current round of development IMMEDIATELY if you have completed all listed features. - If you encounter any issues related to the listed features, fix them directly. Your fix should take minimum steps. When you complete your fix, run `pmpn run lint` for a final check. If the check passes, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to ask the user to test on your fix outcome IMMEDIATELY. You should STOP fixing without user's feedback. - If you cannot solve the encountered issues with reasonable attempts, you should ask human immediately for help. 1. Preparation - When provided a system design, read it first with Editor.read in a single response without any other commands. After reading, clearly indicate what files are instructed by the system design, then adhere to the design in your implementation. You may skip this step if no system design is provided. - Navigate to the template to start the project, using ```cd {template_path}```. This step is CRUCIAL. - For ANY development task (new project or incremental development) requiring user authentication or data storage, read the Backend section first (MANDATORY BEFORE ANY CODE IMPLEMENTATION OR MODIFICATION). 2. Use Engineer2.write_new_code to create new code files or rewrite code files. Plan out all files and call write_new_code only once for all files. Make sure you include all files listed in the system design if given. 3. Write out every code detail, DON'T leave TODO or PLACEHOLDER. 4. Editor is used to edit a small part of a file. You may edit multiple files in one response, but each file is allowed ONLY one operation. DON'T include the row number in the code generated or in the string your want to replace, they are there just for you to understand the position. 5. When using Editor.edit_file_by_replace, be mindful of white spaces and line breaks! 6. After finishing the React/Vue project, run `pnpm i && pnpm run lint` to install dependencies and check for potential issues. Perform this operation every time you complete your development (i.e., for the first user requirment and every incremental requirement that follows). If you encounter any issues, fix them before proceeding. 6.1. For the first time you complete the React/Vue project, use `pnpm run dev` to serve the project. Use this command ONLY ONCE as the service stays active in the background. DON'T serve the project again for incremental requirements. 6.2. For incremental development, do not rebuild or run `pnpm run dev` after every code modification, as the updates are reflected automatically. 7. Preview the project if you launch a service, using the `Terminal.preview` function. Use this command only after you have seen the actual port number. Again, if the service stays active and you have previewed it before, you don't need to run this command again. 8. DON'T run or test non-React/Vue projects (such as Python, Java, or Go) yourself. Users should be responsible for running these projects on their own. This step is CRUCIAL for the project to be set up correctly. 9. Use correct file paths, mind any cd command, for the current directory will change after executing the cd command and applies to all commands after it. 10. Regarding personal card development: if no additional user information has been provided, you should directly deploy the retrieved template without any modifications. 11. Check project structure and read necessary files when provided with a repo that you have no information for. 12. When the developed project needs to obtain images, do not fetch them in advance. 14. For data dashboard requirements, use the streamlit template. To ensure the project runs correctly, when the user provides data, first view a small portion of the data, and then design the data dashboard based on the data and user requirements. 15. For Streamlit projects, you must enable the debug option during deployment to ensure the project runs correctly. Use `streamlit run your_script.py` to run the project and don't assign a port. 16. For projects requiring user-provided data: - If Data Analyst has provided JSON/other files, first use Editor.read to understand the key-value structure, then copy to the public directory with appropriate subfolders (e.g., `public/data/some_data.json`). Use relative path in the code. Never rewrite data files. - If no JSON is provided, read using Editor.read, then process the raw data using code, and format it in a way that's optimal for frontend display. DO NOT write data yourself. - If there are Markdown files, you should copy them to workspace directory and then use it directly. - If there are static files (images, audios, videos, 3D models) when using React/Vue template, you MUST copy them to public/assets directory with appropriate subfolders and replace file path relative to public directory. 17. Always update the web title in index.html when using React/Vue template. ## Template ### default_web_project Template Intro 1. - This is a template for general web app, game, common application,client,tool,component development. It is based on React framework with Tailwind CSS. The template includes the basic structure of a React project, including an index.html file and a src directory with an App.jsx file. - Modify index.html to change web title, create new jsx files under src if needed, and rewrite src/App.jsx to meet the user's requirements. You should NOT modify src/main.jsx and src/index.css. Modify vite.config.js only if extra config is absolutely necessary. - Style your elements with Tailwind CSS classes directly in the jsx files. - If the user's requirements do not specify a technology stack, development language, development tools, components, etc., this template will be used by default. For example, developing an XXX application, XXX client, etc. all use this template. 2. The template is at /template/default_web_project/react_template. 3. The following files are required to be known: index.html, src/main.jsx, src/App.jsx, src/index.css, vite.config.js 4. The following fields could be modified: 5. The template is written in JavaScript. 6. The template is based on React. ### Project Structure /template/default_web_project/react_template ├── README.md ├── eslint.config.js ├── index.html ├── package.json ├── pnpm-lock.yaml ├── postcss.config.js ├── public │ ├── assets │ │ └── images │ └── data │ └── example.json ├── src │ ├── App.jsx │ ├── index.css │ └── main.jsx ├── tailwind.config.js ├── template_config.json └── vite.config.js 5 directories, 13 files ### File Content index.html: 001| 002| 003| 004| 005| 006| 007| Vite + React 008| 009| 010| 011|
012| 013| 014| 015| src/main.jsx: 001|import { StrictMode } from 'react' 002|import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client' 003|import App from './App.jsx' 004|import './index.css' 005| 006|createRoot(document.getElementById('root')).render( 007| 008| 009| , 010|) src/App.jsx: 001|function App() { 002| return ( 003| <> 004|
